title: Living in Boulder Vlog 2 - 2 - 2017: Original talking video from 9 yrs ago in background, request

enrichment.summary: Kyle records a brief daily vlog updating on mundane tasks: he missed making a video the previous night because he was high while working on reorganizing his website's Living in Boulder videos page. He also dealt with car battery issues, helped his mom move a table, and picked up his car from the mechanic. He mentions wanting to write up economist Mark Blyth's ideas for his politics/economics page.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: wants a dedicated Living in Boulder page with stacked videos
thinks his car is worth around $15,000 but needs more work before selling
finds his ideas compelling and wants to explain them with visuals on his politics page

transcript.text: Hello, Living in Boulder vlog from March 2nd. I got a request from Tyne to have one of my videos in the background, my old videos, so I don't know which one. I'll figure out which one later.

But, today, I didn't manage to do my video last night again because I was too, like, working on my videos page. I separated Outliving in Boulder. I want to have that its own page for download or linking to YouTube and adding numbers.
I did it, like, the whole thing, but I did it wrong somehow. I was too high. I must have gotten retarded from being high in that alcohol, but, so I had to do it again.
But, yeah, I mean, because if it has its own page, then that's for your whole life, so you just stack them down and then have the videos on its own page.
But, yeah, I didn't realize it was as basic as it was, so I was just kind of overwhelmed, and so, but, today, I got up.
And I was working on it, and then my mom called me. Oh, no, no, I have to do the video from yesterday.

Yeah, yesterday, my mom called me because her car broke, like, when it starts, she left the batteries and the lights on, so I went down there and took it, and we got the battery replaced, and then I came back here.
And then today, I was working, and then my mom called me. She got this table. It wouldn't fit in her car, so I had to drive down to Louisville with my roommate's car to fit the table in there.
And I drove it back, and then I was working a little bit, not very long, and the mechanic called me, and he said, my car's ready.
So I went down there and got that. He thinks my car's worth $13,000. I was like, what? Yeah, but I'll probably have to sell my motorcycle, too, but I have to finish shampooing it.
I needed some stuff. It didn't dry right. Some stuff got stained and dry out the rug and fix the rocket box.

So I'll do that tomorrow. The rest of the day, I'll just be working away, chopping away at this YouTube stuff, so, and then I'll make videos, like, on my website, and then I'll make videos for my band tomorrow.
Okay.
Yeah, I'm listening to that economist, Mark Bly, that got the idea to, I've just been listening to him over and over, just to get into the mindset.
All of a sudden, um, I, uh, yeah, I want to make an economic, write it my own economic thing on my politics page, on my economics, explain it all, like, what he's saying with the pictures and stuff.
Uh, but,
Ooh,
Ooh,
Ooh,
Ooh,
That'll work. Okay, that's enough for now. Peace out.
